200→    : [];
   201→
   202→  // Build task scope if taskId provided
   203→  let taskScope: TaskScopeContext | undefined;
   204→  if (taskId) {
   205→    taskScope = await buildTaskScope(surreal, workspaceRecord, projectRecord, taskId);
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  return {
   209→    workspace: { id: workspaceRecord.id as string, name: workspaceName },
   210→    project: {
   211→      id: projectRecord.id as string,
   212→      name: project.name,
   213→      status: project.status,
   214→      ...(project.description ? { description: project.description } : {}),
   215→    },
   216→    ...(taskScope ? { task_scope: taskScope } : {}),
   217→    decisions: { confirmed, provisional, contested },
   218→    active_tasks: activeTasks,
   219→    open_questions: openQuestions,
   220→    recent_changes: recentChanges,
   221→    observations,
   222→  };
   223→}
   224→
   225→/** Build task-scoped context: subtasks, parent feature, siblings, dependencies, related sessions */
   226→async function buildTaskScope(
   227→  surreal: Surreal,
   228→  _workspaceRecord: RecordId<"workspace", string>,
   229→  _projectRecord: RecordId<"project", string>,
   230→  taskId: string,
   231→): Promise<TaskScopeContext> {
   232→  const taskRecord = new RecordId("task", taskId);
   233→  const task = await surreal.select<TaskRow>(taskRecord);
   234→  if (!task) {
   235→    throw new Error(`task not found: ${taskId}`);
   236→  }
   237→
   238→  // Load subtasks, parent feature, dependencies, and related sessions in parallel
   239→  const taskScopeQuery = `
   240→    -- Subtasks of this task
   241→    SELECT id, title, status
   242→    FROM task
   243→    WHERE id IN (SELECT VALUE \`in\` FROM subtask_of WHERE out = $task)
   244→    ORDER BY created_at ASC;
   245→
   246→    -- Parent feature (via has_task edge)
   247→    SELECT id, name, description
   248→    FROM feature
   249→    WHERE id IN (SELECT VALUE \`in\` FROM has_task WHERE out = $task)
   250→    LIMIT 1;
   251→
   252→    -- Dependencies (tasks this task depends on)
   253→    SELECT id, title, status
   254→    FROM task
   255→    WHERE id IN (SELECT VALUE out FROM depends_on WHERE \`in\` = $task);
   256→
   257→    -- Recent agent sessions that touched this task
   258→    SELECT id, agent, ended_at, summary
   259→    FROM agent_session
   260→    WHERE task_id = $task AND ended_at != NONE
   261→    ORDER BY ended_at DESC LIMIT 5;
   262→  `;
   263→
   264→  const results = await surreal
   265→    .query<[SubtaskRow[], FeatureRow[], DependencyRow[], AgentSessionRow[]]>(taskScopeQuery, {
   266→      task: taskRecord,
   267→    })
   268→    .collect<[SubtaskRow[], FeatureRow[], DependencyRow[], AgentSessionRow[]]>();
   269→
   270→  const [subtaskRows, featureRows, dependencyRows, sessionRows] = results;
   271→
   272→  const parentFeature = featureRows.length > 0
   273→    ? {
   274→        id: toId(featureRows[0].id),
   275→        name: featureRows[0].name,
   276→        ...(featureRows[0].description ? { description: featureRows[0].description } : {}),
   277→      }
   278→    : undefined;
   279→
   280→  // Load sibling tasks (other tasks under same feature)
   281→  let siblingTasks: { id: string; title: string; status: string }[] = [];
   282→  if (parentFeature && featureRows.length > 0) {
   283→    const [siblingRows] = await surreal
   284→      .query<[TaskRow[]]>(
   285→        `SELECT id, title, status FROM task
   286→         WHERE id IN (SELECT VALUE out FROM has_task WHERE \`in\` = $feature)
   287→           AND id != $task
   288→         ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 20;`,
   289→        { feature: featureRows[0].id, task: taskRecord },
   290→      )
   291→      .collect<[TaskRow[]]>();
   292→
   293→    siblingTasks = siblingRows.map((t) => ({
   294→      id: toId(t.id),
   295→      title: t.title,
   296→      status: t.status,
   297→    }));
   298→  }
   299→
   300→  return {
   301→    task: {
   302→      id: toId(task.id),
   303→      title: task.title,
   304→      ...(task.description ? { description: task.description } : {}),
   305→      status: task.status,
   306→      ...(task.category ? { category: task.category } : {}),
   307→    },
   308→    subtasks: subtaskRows.map((s) => ({
   309→      id: toId(s.id),
   310→      title: s.title,
   311→      status: s.status,
   312→    })),
   313→    parent_feature: parentFeature,
   314→    sibling_tasks: siblingTasks,
   315→    dependencies: dependencyRows.map((d) => ({
   316→      id: toId(d.id),
   317→      title: d.title,
   318→      status: d.status,
   319→    })),
   320→    related_sessions: sessionRows.map((s) => ({
   321→      id: toId(s.id),
   322→      agent: s.agent,
   323→      ended_at: toIso(s.ended_at),
   324→      summary: s.summary ?? "",
   325→    })),
   326→  };
   327→}
   328→
   329→/** Load entities changed since a timestamp for the recent_changes field */
   330→async function loadRecentChanges(
   331→  surreal: Surreal,
   332→  workspaceRecord: RecordId<"workspace", string>,
   333→  projectRecord: RecordId<"project", string>,
   334→  since: string,
   335→): Promise<RecentChangeContext[]> {
   336→  const sinceDate = new Date(since);
   337→
   338→  const query = `
   339→    LET $project_entity_ids = SELECT VALUE \`in\` FROM belongs_to WHERE out = $project;
   340→
   341→    LET $recent_decisions = SELECT "decision" AS entity_type, summary AS entity_name, status AS change_type, updated_at AS changed_at
   342→    FROM decision
   343→    WHERE workspace = $workspace AND id IN $project_entity_ids AND updated_at > $since
   344→    ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 20;
   345→
   346→    LET $recent_tasks = SELECT "task" AS entity_type, title AS entity_name, status AS change_type, updated_at AS changed_at
   347→    FROM task
   348→    WHERE workspace = $workspace AND id IN $project_entity_ids AND updated_at > $since
   349→    ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 20;

   865→    const truncatedDiff = body.diff.length > 8000 ? body.diff.slice(0, 8000) + "\n... (truncated)" : body.diff;
   866→
   867→    const result = await generateObject({
   868→      model: extractionModel,
   869→      schema: commitCheckSchema,
   870→      temperature: 0.1,
   871→      system: [
   872→        "You are a pre-commit analyzer for a knowledge graph-integrated development workflow.",
   873→        "Analyze the staged git diff and commit message against the project context.",
   874→        "Detect:",
   875→        "1. Task completions — does this commit complete or substantially finish any active tasks?",
   876→        "2. Unlogged decisions — does this commit introduce architectural or design decisions not already in the knowledge graph?",
   877→        "3. Constraint violations — does this commit contradict any confirmed decisions or active constraints?",
   878→        "Be conservative: only flag items with genuine evidence in the diff. Empty arrays are fine if nothing is detected.",
   879→      ].join("\n"),
   880→      prompt: [
   881→        "## Commit message",
   882→        body.commit_message || "(no message)",
   883→        "",
   884→        "## Staged diff",
   885→        truncatedDiff,
   886→        "",
   887→        "## Active tasks",
   888→        activeTasks.length > 0
   889→          ? activeTasks.map((t) => `- [${t.status}] ${t.title}`).join("\n")
   890→          : "(no active tasks)",
   891→        "",
   892→        "## Recent decisions",
   893→        (() => {
   894→          const allDecisions = [...decisions.confirmed, ...decisions.provisional, ...decisions.contested];

   896→            ? allDecisions.map((d) => `- [${d.status}] ${d.summary}`).join("\n")
   897→            : "(no decisions)";
   898→        })(),
   899→        "",
   900→        "## Active constraints",
   901→        constraints.length > 0
   902→          ? constraints.map((c) => `- [${c.severity}] ${c.text}`).join("\n")
   903→          : "(no constraints)",
   904→      ].join("\n"),
   905→    });

content.text:
I need to add a `source_session` field to the `task` table in a SurrealDB-backed project so we can see which coding agent session is currently working on a task.

## Current State

**Task table** (`schema/surreal-schema.surql` lines 92-119):
- Already has `status ON task TYPE string` (unconstrained)
- Status values used: "open" (work-item-accept, create-work-item), "todo" (subtask creation), "in_progress" (auto-set when agent session starts), "ready", "blocked", "done", "completed"
- No `source_session` field exists on task

**Observation table** already has this pattern implemented:
- Schema: `DEFINE FIELD source_session ON observation TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;`
- Code: `...(input.sourceSessionRecord ? { source_session: input.sourceSessionRecord } : {})`

**Agent session start** (`app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` lines 485-509):
- `createAgentSession()` creates the session record
- If task-scoped, auto-updates task status to "in_progress" 
- This is the natural place to also set `source_session` on the task

**Agent session end** (`app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` line 512+):
- `endAgentSession()` populates session summary and creates edges
- Should clear `source_session` from the task when session ends

**Task creation paths:**
1. `app/src/server/entities/work-item-accept-route.ts` - creates with status "open"
2. `app/src/server/chat/tools/create-work-item.ts` - creates with status "open"  
3. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` `createSubtask()` - creates with status "todo"

**Task queries in context builder** (`app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts` line 285):
- Queries `SELECT id, title, status FROM task` for sibling tasks
- Could optionally include `source_session` to show which agent is working on it

**Existing migrations** in `schema/migrations/`:
- 20260304_agent_session_and_subtask.surql
- 20260304_fulltext_search_indexes.surql
- 20260304_mcp_auth_and_question_fields.surql
- 20260304_observation_coding_agent_fields.surql

## Requirements
1. Add `source_session` field to task table (optional record<agent_session>)
2. Set it when an agent session starts with a task scope
3. Clear it when the agent session ends
4. The status field already exists - user likely wants it formalized with an ASSERT constraint

## Conventions
- Project does NOT maintain backwards compatibility - schema changes are breaking
- New fields should be required from the start (but this is optional since not all tasks have sessions)
- Use DEFINE ... OVERWRITE or ALTER TABLE/FIELD for schema evolution
- Migration scripts go in `schema/migrations/` with date prefix
- Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;
- Apply via `bun migrate`

Please design the implementation plan including:
1. Migration script
2. Code changes to set/clear source_session
3. Any query changes to expose the field

I need to add a `source_session` field to the `task` table in a SurrealDB-backed project so we can see which coding agent session is currently working on a task.

## Current State

**Task table** (`schema/surreal-schema.surql` lines 92-119):
- Already has `status ON task TYPE string` (unconstrained)
- Status values used: "open" (work-item-accept, create-work-item), "todo" (subtask creation), "in_progress" (auto-set when agent session starts), "ready", "blocked", "done", "completed"
- No `source_session` field exists on task

**Observation table** already has this pattern implemented:
- Schema: `DEFINE FIELD source_session ON observation TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;`
- Code: `...(input.sourceSessionRecord ? { source_session: input.sourceSessionRecord } : {})`

**Agent session start** (`app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` lines 485-509):
- `createAgentSession()` creates the session record
- If task-scoped, auto-updates task status to "in_progress" 
- This is the natural place to also set `source_session` on the task

**Agent session end** (`app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` line 512+):
- `endAgentSession()` populates session summary and creates edges
- Should clear `source_session` from the task when session ends

**Task creation paths:**
1. `app/src/server/entities/work-item-accept-route.ts` - creates with status "open"
2. `app/src/server/chat/tools/create-work-item.ts` - creates with status "open"  
3. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` `createSubtask()` - creates with status "todo"

**Task queries in context builder** (`app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts` line 285):
- Queries `SELECT id, title, status FROM task` for sibling tasks
- Could optionally include `source_session` to show which agent is working on it

**Task table** (`schema/surreal-schema.surql` lines 92-119):
- Already has `status ON task TYPE string` (unconstrained)
- Status values used: "open" (work-item-accept, create-work-item), "todo" (subtask creation), "in_progress" (auto-set when agent session starts), "ready", "blocked", "done", "completed"
- No `source_session` field exists on task

**Observation table** already has this pattern implemented:
- Schema: `DEFINE FIELD source_session ON observation TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;`
- Code: `...(input.sourceSessionRecord ? { source_session: input.sourceSessionRecord } : {})`

**Agent session start** (`app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` lines 485-509):
- `createAgentSession()` creates the session record
- If task-scoped, auto-updates task status to "in_progress" 
- This is the natural place to also set `source_session` on the task

**Agent session end** (`app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` line 512+):
- `endAgentSession()` populates session summary and creates edges
- Should clear `source_session` from the task when session ends

**Task creation paths:**
1. `app/src/server/entities/work-item-accept-route.ts` - creates with status "open"
2. `app/src/server/chat/tools/create-work-item.ts` - creates with status "open"  
3. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` `createSubtask()` - creates with status "todo"

**Task queries in context builder** (`app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts` line 285):
- Queries `SELECT id, title, status FROM task` for sibling tasks
- Could optionally include `source_session` to show which agent is working on it

**Existing migrations** in `schema/migrations/`:
- 20260304_agent_session_and_subtask.surql
- 20260304_fulltext_search_indexes.surql
- 20260304_mcp_auth_and_question_fields.surql
- 20260304_observation_coding_agent_fields.surql

## Requirements
1. Add `source_session` field to task table (optional record<agent_session>)
2. Set it when an agent session starts with a task scope
3. Clear it when the agent session ends
4. The status field already exists - user likely wants it formalized with an ASSERT constraint

## Conventions
- Project does NOT maintain backwards compatibility - schema changes are breaking
- New fields should be required from the start (but this is optional since not all tasks have sessions)
- Use DEFINE ... OVERWRITE or ALTER TABLE/FIELD for schema evolution
- Migration scripts go in `schema/migrations/` with date prefix
- Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;
- Apply via `bun migrate`

Please design the implementation plan including:
1. Migration script
2. Code changes to set/clear source_session
3. Any query changes to expose the field

- `createAgentSession()` creates the session record
- If task-scoped, auto-updates task status to "in_progress" 
- This is the natural place to also set `source_session` on the task

**Agent session end** (`app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` line 512+):
- `endAgentSession()` populates session summary and creates edges
- Should clear `source_session` from the task when session ends

message.content: why would we clear the source_session?

content.text:
Good question. You're right — clearing `source_session` on session end doesn't make sense. The value of `source_session` is showing *which* session last worked on (or is working on) the task. Clearing it loses that information.

The session already has `ended_at` to indicate whether it's still active, so anyone querying can check `source_session.ended_at` to distinguish "actively being worked on" from "last worked on by".

I'll remove step 4 (clearing on session end) from the plan.

file.content:
# Plan: Add `source_session` field to task table

## Context

Coding agent sessions already track which task they're working on via `agent_session.task_id`, but there's no reverse link — given a task, you can't see which agent session is currently working on it. Adding `source_session` to the `task` table enables at-a-glance visibility into which agent is working on each task and its progress status.

The `observation` table already implements this exact pattern (`source_session: option<record<agent_session>>`), so we follow the same convention.

## Changes

### 1. Migration script

**New file:** `schema/migrations/20260304_task_source_session.surql`

```sql
BEGIN TRANSACTION;
DEFINE FIELD source_session ON task TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;
ALTER FIELD status ON task TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["open", "todo", "ready", "in_progress", "blocked", "done", "completed"];
COMMIT TRANSACTION;
```

### 2. Update canonical schema

**File:** `schema/surreal-schema.surql` (line ~117, after `workspace` field)

Add: `DEFINE FIELD source_session ON task TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;`

Update status field (line 102) to add ASSERT:
```sql
DEFINE FIELD status ON task TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["open", "todo", "ready", "in_progress", "blocked", "done", "completed"];
```

### 3. Set `source_session` when agent session starts with task scope

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` — `createAgentSession()` (~line 498)

When a task-scoped session starts, set `source_session` on the task to the new session record. Merge into the existing task update block:

```typescript
if (input.taskId) {
  const taskRecord = new RecordId("task", input.taskId);
  const task = await input.surreal.select<TaskRow>(taskRecord);
  if (task) {
    await input.surreal.update(taskRecord).merge({
      ...(task.status === "todo" || task.status === "ready"
        ? { status: "in_progress" }
        : {}),
      source_session: sessionRecord,
      updated_at: now,
    });
  }
}
```

### 4. Clear `source_session` when agent session ends

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` — `endAgentSession()` (~line 548, after session update)

Use a conditional clear to handle concurrent sessions safely (only clear if this session still owns the task):

```typescript
// Clear source_session from the associated task (only if this session owns it)
const session = await input.surreal.select<{ task_id?: RecordId<"task", string> }>(sessionRecord);
if (session?.task_id) {
  await input.surreal.query(
    "UPDATE $task SET source_session = NONE, updated_at = $now WHERE source_session = $session;",
    { task: session.task_id, now, session: sessionRecord },
  );
}
```

### 5. Surface `source_session` in context queries

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/types.ts`

- Add `active_session?: string` to `TaskContext` (line 44)
- Add `active_session?: string` to sibling task shape in `TaskScopeContext` (line 29)

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts`

- Add `source_session` to task SELECT queries (~line 285 siblings, ~line 116 project tasks)
- Map `source_session` → `active_session` in output objects

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts` (~line 859)

- Add `source_session` to the active tasks query in check-commit handler

## Files touched

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `schema/migrations/20260304_task_source_session.surql` | New |
| `schema/surreal-schema.surql` | Add field |
| `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` | Set on session start, clear on session end |
| `app/src/server/mcp/types.ts` | Add `active_session` to types |
| `app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts` | Add to queries + mappings |
| `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts` | Add to check-commit task query |

## Verification

1. `bun migrate` — applies the migration
2. Start a task-scoped agent session → verify `source_session` is set on the task
3. End the session → verify `source_session` is cleared
4. Check context packet includes `active_session` for tasks with active sessions

## Context

Coding agent sessions already track which task they're working on via `agent_session.task_id`, but there's no reverse link — given a task, you can't see which agent session is currently working on it. Adding `source_session` to the `task` table enables at-a-glance visibility into which agent is working on each task and its progress status.

The `observation` table already implements this exact pattern (`source_session: option<record<agent_session>>`), so we follow the same convention.

## Changes

### 1. Migration script

**New file:** `schema/migrations/20260304_task_source_session.surql`

```sql
BEGIN TRANSACTION;
DEFINE FIELD source_session ON task TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;
ALTER FIELD status ON task TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["open", "todo", "ready", "in_progress", "blocked", "done", "completed"];
COMMIT TRANSACTION;
```

### 2. Update canonical schema

**File:** `schema/surreal-schema.surql` (line ~117, after `workspace` field)

Add: `DEFINE FIELD source_session ON task TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;`

Update status field (line 102) to add ASSERT:
```sql
DEFINE FIELD status ON task TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["open", "todo", "ready", "in_progress", "blocked", "done", "completed"];
```

### 3. Set `source_session` when agent session starts with task scope

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` — `createAgentSession()` (~line 498)

When a task-scoped session starts, set `source_session` on the task to the new session record. Merge into the existing task update block:

```typescript
if (input.taskId) {
  const taskRecord = new RecordId("task", input.taskId);
  const task = await input.surreal.select<TaskRow>(taskRecord);
  if (task) {
    await input.surreal.update(taskRecord).merge({
      ...(task.status === "todo" || task.status === "ready"
        ? { status: "in_progress" }
        : {}),
      source_session: sessionRecord,
      updated_at: now,

  }
}
```

### 4. Clear `source_session` when agent session ends

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` — `endAgentSession()` (~line 548, after session update)

```typescript
const session = await input.surreal.select<{ task_id?: RecordId<"task", string> }>(sessionRecord);
if (session?.task_id) {
  await input.surreal.query(
    "UPDATE $task SET source_session = NONE, updated_at = $now WHERE source_session = $session;",
    { task: session.task_id, now, session: sessionRecord },
  );
}
```

- Add `source_session` to the active tasks query in check-commit handler

## Files touched

# Plan: Add `source_session` field to task table

## Context

Coding agent sessions already track which task they're working on via `agent_session.task_id`, but there's no reverse link — given a task, you can't see which agent session is currently working on it. Adding `source_session` to the `task` table enables at-a-glance visibility into which agent is working on each task and its progress status.

The `observation` table already implements this exact pattern (`source_session: option<record<agent_session>>`), so we follow the same convention.

## Changes

### 1. Migration script

**New file:** `schema/migrations/20260304_task_source_session.surql`

```sql
BEGIN TRANSACTION;
DEFINE FIELD source_session ON task TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;
ALTER FIELD status ON task TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["open", "todo", "ready", "in_progress", "blocked", "done", "completed"];
COMMIT TRANSACTION;
```

### 2. Update canonical schema

**File:** `schema/surreal-schema.surql` (line ~117, after `workspace` field)

Add: `DEFINE FIELD source_session ON task TYPE option<record<agent_session>>;`

Update status field (line 102) to add ASSERT:
```sql
DEFINE FIELD status ON task TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["open", "todo", "ready", "in_progress", "blocked", "done", "completed"];
```

### 3. Set `source_session` when agent session starts with task scope

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` — `createAgentSession()` (~line 498)

When a task-scoped session starts, set `source_session` on the task to the new session record. Merge into the existing task update block:

```typescript
if (input.taskId) {
  const taskRecord = new RecordId("task", input.taskId);
  const task = await input.surreal.select<TaskRow>(taskRecord);
  if (task) {
    await input.surreal.update(taskRecord).merge({
      ...(task.status === "todo" || task.status === "ready"
        ? { status: "in_progress" }
        : {}),
      source_session: sessionRecord,
      updated_at: now,
    });
  }
}
```

### 4. Surface `source_session` in context queries

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/types.ts`

- Add `active_session?: string` to `TaskContext` (line 44)
- Add `active_session?: string` to sibling task shape in `TaskScopeContext` (line 29)

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts`

- Add `source_session` to task SELECT queries (~line 285 siblings, ~line 116 project tasks)
- Map `source_session` → `active_session` in output objects

**File:** `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts` (~line 859)

- Add `source_session` to the active tasks query in check-commit handler

## Files touched

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `schema/migrations/20260304_task_source_session.surql` | New |
| `schema/surreal-schema.surql` | Add field |
| `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` | Set on session start, clear on session end |
| `app/src/server/mcp/types.ts` | Add `active_session` to types |
| `app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts` | Add to queries + mappings |
| `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts` | Add to check-commit task query |

## Verification

1. `bun migrate` — applies the migration
2. Start a task-scoped agent session → verify `source_session` is set on the task
3. End the session → verify `source_session` is cleared
4. Check context packet includes `active_session` for tasks with active sessions

how would we automatically relate tasks to commits ?

input.thought:
The user is asking about automatically relating tasks to commits. Let me think about the existing infrastructure:

1. There's already a `logCommit` function in mcp-queries.ts that creates git_commit records
2. There's a check-commit handler in mcp-route.ts that uses LLM analysis on staged diffs
3. The agent session tracks `task_id` - so when a session ends, we know which task was being worked on
4. The `source_commit` field already exists on task table

The question is: how to automatically create the link. The most natural approach is: when a coding agent commits while in a task-scoped session, the commit gets linked to that task automatically. The session already knows the task_id.

Let me think about the current flow:
- Agent session starts with task_id → task gets source_session set
- Agent works, makes commits
- check-commit hook fires → POST /api/mcp/:workspaceId/commits/check (LLM analysis)
- commit happens → POST /api/mcp/:workspaceId/commits (logCommit)

The logCommit function already accepts taskUpdates, but the linkage between "which task is this commit for" needs to come from somewhere. If the session is task-scoped, the commit should automatically be related to that task.

The cleanest approach: when logging a commit, if there's an active session (session_id passed in the request), look up the session's task_id and create the relation automatically.

content.content: {"thoughtNumber":1,"totalThoughts":1,"nextThoughtNeeded":false,"branches":[],"thoughtHistoryLength":1}

content.text: The infrastructure is mostly already there. Let me check the current commit flow to show the exact connection point.

Here's the picture. There are two natural connection points:

**1. Session-based (already mostly wired):** When a commit happens during a task-scoped session, the `session_id` is available. `logCommit` just needs to accept an optional `session_id`, look up the session's `task_id`, and create a `RELATE task->implemented_by->git_commit` edge automatically.

**2. Hook-based (the `check-commit` path):** The pre-commit LLM analysis already loads active tasks and detects `task_completions` with confidence scores. This could feed directly into `logCommit` — the hook fires before commit, identifies which task the commit relates to, then the post-commit `logCommit` call creates the relation.

The simplest approach that requires minimal new code:

```
post-commit hook fires
  → POST /commits with session_id
    → logCommit looks up session.task_id
    → RELATE task -> implemented_by -> git_commit
    → done
```

No LLM needed for the linkage — the session already knows the task. The LLM analysis in `check-commit` is a bonus for detecting *additional* task completions beyond the scoped one.

Want me to implement this? It's essentially:
1. Add `session_id?: string` to the `logCommit` input
2. If present, look up the session's `task_id` and create the relation edge
3. Pass `session_id` from the commit hook request body

message.content: but a session can work on multiple tasks at the same time ??

content.thinking:
The user is questioning whether a session can work on multiple tasks. Let me check the current model.

Looking at the `agent_session` table:
- `task_id ON agent_session TYPE option<record<task>>` — singular, optional

And `createAgentSession` takes `taskId?: string` — singular.

So the current model is: one session → one task (or no task). But the user is pointing out that in reality, a coding agent session might touch multiple tasks — e.g., fixing a bug in one task while implementing a feature for another, or working on subtasks.

For the commit-to-task linkage, this means we can't just rely on session.task_id to know which task a commit relates to. 

A commit could actually span multiple tasks, or the agent might not be task-scoped at all, which undermines the whole approach of using session_id as the primary way to link commits back to tasks.

The user's pointing out something real here — a session is already designed to touch multiple tasks (there's even a `tasks_progressed` array), so relying on just `session.task_id` would miss commits related to tasks the agent worked on but wasn't directly scoped to. Better approaches would be analyzing the commit message and diff directly, leveraging the `tasks_progressed` data we're already tracking, or having the agent explicitly declare which tasks each commit relates to.

content.text:
You're right. Looking at the current model:

- `agent_session.task_id` is singular (`option<record<task>>`) — the "scoped" task
- But `tasks_progressed` is already an array — acknowledging a session touches multiple tasks
- An agent can call `create_subtask`, `update_task_status` on any task mid-session

So session→task is really many-to-many in practice, and relying on `session.task_id` for commit linkage would only capture the primary scoped task, missing everything else.

For automatic commit→task linkage, the more reliable approaches would be:

1. **Agent declares it explicitly** — the `logCommit` API already accepts `task_updates[]`. The agent (or its hooks) lists which tasks the commit relates to. No guessing.

2. **LLM matching at commit time** — the `check-commit` handler already loads active tasks and produces `task_completions[]` with confidence scores. Feed those matches into the subsequent `logCommit` call to create the edges.

3. **Track at the action level** — every `update_task_status` or `create_subtask` call during a session already knows the task ID. A post-commit hook could correlate "which tasks were touched since the last commit" by timestamp.

Option 1 is the most straightforward and already half-wired. The agent knows what it worked on. Do you want to lean into that, or prefer the LLM-matching approach where the system infers it from the diff?

Now I see the full picture. The existing `implemented_by` relation only goes `decision -> git_commit | pull_request`. We need a relation for `task -> git_commit` as well. Let me think about the right approach:

1. Add `task_ids?: string[]` to the `logCommit` input — the list of tasks this commit relates to
2. Create edges linking tasks to the commit
3. Update the route handler to pass through `task_ids` from the request body

For the relation, I could either:
- Extend `implemented_by` to also allow `IN task` (currently only `IN decision`)


- Create a new relation type like `committed_for`

Looking at the schema, `implemented_by` is `IN decision OUT git_commit | pull_request`. I could extend it to `IN decision | task OUT git_commit | pull_request`, or create a separate relation. The semantic difference is that `implemented_by` means "this decision was implemented by this commit," while for tasks it's more like "this commit progressed/contributed to this task." A different relation name would be clearer, but I could also just reuse `implemented_by` for both — a task can be "implemented by" a commit just as well.

Actually, the simplest approach is to add a `related_task_ids` field to the `logCommit` input, then create relation edges from each task to the commit when processing those IDs.

For the relation type, I'm thinking of extending `implemented_by` to allow tasks as the source (currently it's `IN decision OUT git_commit | pull_request`), which keeps the schema clean without introducing a new relation table. I'll need a migration to update the relation definition, but that's straightforward.

The key insight is that the current `task_updates` API requires a status change, but we really just need a way to link commits to related tasks without forcing a status update. So I'm adding an optional `related_task_ids` field to the request body that lets the agent specify which tasks a commit relates to, and then I'll create edges connecting each task to the commit.
